title: Scheduler supervision integration — exit signal and DOWN propagation
purpose: The supervision data structures work correctly in isolation but the scheduler's cleanup_exited_process() does not call them. Process death silently discards links and monitors. This brief wires the scheduler to the supervision layer so process death propagates signals, completing the OTP supervision loop.
task: Modify cleanup_exited_process() to call LinkSet::process_exited() and MonitorSet::process_exited(). Add LinkSet and MonitorSet to SharedState. Ensure woken processes move from wait set to run queue. Handle cascade iteratively.
boundaries: This brief SHALL NOT implement supervisor behaviours (one_for_one, one_for_all, rest_for_one — those are Gleam library code per ADR-007)
This brief SHALL NOT implement application controllers or application start/stop
This brief SHALL NOT implement process registry cleanup on exit (erlang:unregister is out of scope)

requirements.title: SharedState holds LinkSet and MonitorSet
requirements.spec: SharedState SHALL contain LinkSet and MonitorSet wrapped in Mutex. Scheduler::new() SHALL initialize both as empty.
acceptance: SharedState contains link_set: Mutex<LinkSet> and monitor_set: Mutex<MonitorSet>
Scheduler::new() initializes both as empty
Existing scheduler tests continue to pass

requirements.title: cleanup_exited_process propagates exit signals
requirements.spec: When a process exits, cleanup_exited_process() SHALL take links, deliver exit signals, terminate non-trapping linked processes, enqueue EXIT messages for trapping processes, and wake waiting processes. Cascade SHALL be iterative via worklist pattern.
acceptance: Linked process receives exit signal when partner dies
Process with trap_exit=true receives {EXIT, Pid, Reason} message
Process without trap_exit dies from non-normal linked exit
Normal exit does not kill linked processes
Kill signal terminates regardless of trap_exit
Killed process propagates killed not kill
Cascade: A links B links C, A dies -> B dies -> C dies
Waiting process woken by EXIT message moves to run queue

requirements.title: cleanup_exited_process delivers DOWN messages
requirements.spec: When a process exits, cleanup_exited_process() SHALL query MonitorSet for watchers, construct {DOWN, Ref, process, Pid, Reason} on watcher heap, enqueue in mailbox, remove monitor, and wake waiting watchers.
acceptance: Monitored process exit delivers DOWN to watcher
DOWN message contains correct Ref, Pid, and Reason
Watcher in wait set is woken by DOWN message
Multiple monitors deliver independent DOWN messages
Demonitored target death does not send DOWN

requirements.title: BIF integration with SharedState
requirements.spec: Process BIFs from B-023 SHALL route through SharedState's LinkSet and MonitorSet. ProcessContext SHALL carry a reference to shared supervision state.
acceptance: link/1 BIF adds to SharedState LinkSet
unlink/1 BIF removes from SharedState LinkSet
monitor/2 BIF adds to SharedState MonitorSet
demonitor/1 BIF removes from SharedState MonitorSet
spawn_link/3 atomically spawns and links via SharedState

requirements.title: Integration tests
requirements.spec: End-to-end tests SHALL verify the complete supervision loop on the scheduler: spawn, link/monitor, exit, verify signal/DOWN delivery.
acceptance: Test: spawn + link + error exit -> linked process dies
Test: spawn + link + normal exit -> linked process survives
Test: spawn + link + trap_exit -> {EXIT, Pid, Reason} received
Test: spawn + link + kill -> bypasses trap_exit, propagates killed
Test: spawn + monitor + exit -> {DOWN, Ref, process, Pid, Reason} received
All tests run on the scheduler, not unit-level
